title: THE ONE WITH RACHEL'S PHONE NUMBER

lines.speaker: Transcript by
lines.text: David Buehrle
Chandler's office in Tulsa.   His assistant is showing him photographs.

lines.speaker: Jo Lynn
lines.text: This kitty is Mittens and this one is Fitzhugh, and this little guy in the cat condo is Jinkies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yep.  That's a lot of cats Jo Lynn.  Single are ya?
Phone rings.  Chandler answers using the speaker phone.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Chandler Bing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.  How come you're answering your own phone?  Where's your crazy assistant?
She frowns.  Chandler picks up the hand set.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's up Joe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, what have we always wanted to do together?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Braid each other's hair and ride horseback on the beach?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no.  When you get home tomorrow night, you and I are going to be at the Wizzards-Knicks game . . .  courtside!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Courtside?  Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.  Maybe Michael Jordon will dive for the ball and break my jaw with his knee.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's so cool.  I'll let Monica know.
Chandler hangs up and calls Monica who is reading a book on their sofa as the phone rings.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey just called.  He's got courtside Knicks tickets for him and me tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?  But tomorrow night is the only night I get off from the restaurant.  If you go to the game, we won't have a night together for another week.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But hey, it's courtside.  The cheerleaders are going to be right in fr. . . (Pause)  That's not the way to convince you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler look, I don't want to be one of those wives who says, "You can't go to the game.  You have to spend time with me."  So, if you could just realize it on your own . . .

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know.  You're right.  I want to see you too.  I've just got to figure out a way to tell Joey, you know?  He's really looking forward to this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Tell him that you haven't seen your wife in a long time.  Tell him that having a long-distance relationship is really difficult.  Tell him that what little time we have is precious.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, ah, ah . . .  I'll think of something.
[ OPENING CREDITS ]
Ross and Rachel's apartment.  Ross is in the living room covering Emma.  Rachel enters wearing a sexy dress.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!  (pause)  Wow, You look . . . uh . . .   It's just, ah . . .   That dress . . . uh . . .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I hope the ends of these sentences are good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, well, they're good.  It's been a while since I've seen you like this.  You, you clean up good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh well, well thank you.  (She laughs.  He stares for a moment.)  Okay, stop.  Stop looking at me like that.  The last time that happened, (points to Ross) that happened.  (points to Emma.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh right, right.  (They pause and exchange a glance. Then, Ross looks away.)  So, are you . . . ah . . . you excited about your, your first night away from Emma?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah.  Phoebe and I are going to have so much fun.  And thank you for watching the baby, by the way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, it's fine.  Actually, I, I invited Mike over.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe's Mike?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I didn't know you guys hung out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We don't.  But I thought it would be nice to get to know him.  You know, maybe have a little dinner, drinks, conversation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh that's so cute:  Ross and Mike's first date.  Is that going to be awkward?  I mean, what are you guys going to talk about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know.  But, you know, we, we have a lot in common, you know.  He plays piano; I played keyboards in college.  He's been divorced; I have some experience in that area.
Rachel nods.  There is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.
Phoebe and Mike enter.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (spotting Rachel's dress) Oooh.  Girl's night out indeed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross) Ok.  So now, I think Emma is probably down for the night, but if you need anything Ross . . .

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, Rach, we'll be fine, all right?  You go have fun.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.  You too.  And I hope you score.  (to Mike) Bye.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So . . .  Welcome.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (Holds up a six pack of Foster's Lager) I got beer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I got bottled breast milk.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Eh, why don't we start with the beer?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.  (They sit.)  So, um, Phoebe tells me you, ah, you play piano.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, I, I used to, ah, play keyboards in college.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ah?  (pause)  Do you have one here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Okay.  (pause)
They clink beer bottles, and drink.  Then, they stare uncomfortably at their bottles.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um . . . ah . . . you know, I'm divorced.  Um, Phoebe, ah . . . Phoebe said you . . . You've been divorced?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah.  (pause)  Yeah, I'm sorry.  I don't . . . I don't really like to talk about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pause) That's okay.  We'll talk about (pause) something else.  (They pause.  They drink.)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So, you're a paleontologist, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: My cousin's a paleontologist.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah?  (Mike nods.  Another pause.)  Well, he and I would probably have a lot to talk about.
[Scene:  Monica and Chandler's apartment.  Monica is wearing a sexy negligee.  She pours two glasses of wine as Chandler enters with a carry-on suitcase.  He sets the case by the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Welcome home.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Hangs his jacket over the suitcase, locks the door, then turns to Monica.)  Oh well, look at you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.  What do you think?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it looks great.  It's just that . . . well, I'm wearing the same thing underneath.  So . . .

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See what I mean . . . (They kiss.)
There is a loud thud at the door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!  How come the door's locked?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just a second.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (hushed) No, no, no, no, no.  Joey can't know that I'm here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because I didn't know how to tell him that I couldn't go to the Knicks game.  So, I just told him that I had to stay in Tulsa.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, you lied to him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Achhh.  It's always better to lie than to have the complicated discussion.  (pause)  Except with you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!  Open the door.  What's going on?  (He knocks.)
Monica goes to the door.  Chandler slides behind it as she opens it slightly.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you . ..  (He sees her in her negligee.)  Why are you dressed like that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, because, um . . .  well, Chandler's going to be home in a couple of days.  So, I thought I would, you know, practice the art of seduction.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I thought I heard a man's voice before.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh I was just doing Chandler's side of the conversation.  You know, like, "Hi, How do I look?"  (As Chandler) "Really sexy.  Could I BE any more turned on?"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.  (pause.  He looks over her shoulder at the table with the wine.)  Whoa, whoa.  Why are there two glasses of wine out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because. . .  one of them is for you.
Monica turns to get the wine.  Chandler peeps through the peephole.  Joey, seeing something, peeps back.  Chandler ducks.  Monica returns with two glasses of wine.  She gives one to Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Cheers.  (She clinks his glass and pulls back.)  Okay, buh-bye.  (She closes the door.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, it's funny.  I've been, ah, practicing the art of seduction myself.  (He raises his hands in front of himself, sticks out his behind, and wiggles it.)  Hi ya.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You might want to keep practicing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yah.
Chandler's cell phone rings.  He looks at it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's Joey.  (He answers it, holding it out so that Monica can hear too.)  Hey Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, come home!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What? Why?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: COME . . .  HOME.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look I, I can't.  What's going on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know how to tell you this but, uh . . . I think Monica's cheatin' on ya.  I told you shouldn't have married someone so much hotter than you.
Chandler and Monica look at each other.  Chandler nods.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right look.  (He walks to the hallway.)  If you can't come home and deal with this, then I'm gonna.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: NO!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (outside the apartment door) I just heard him!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (softly) Can you . . . hear him . . . now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (listens at the door.)  No.  (pause) All right, I'm going in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! Wait!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I heard him again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (writhes as if in agony) All right, look.  Just stay there.  I'm coming home.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.  Great.  I'll see you when you get here.  I'm gonna wait out in the hall in case the dude comes out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is that really necessary?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Absolutely.  You'd do it for me.  Not that you ever have to because I know how to keep my women satisfied.
[Scene: Ross and Rachel's apartment.  Mike is blowing a note from his beer bottle.  Ross stares off to the side.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Shouldn't the pizza be here by now?  I mean, they said thirty minutes or less.  Well, how long has it been?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (looking at his watch) Eleven minutes.  (long pause)  And now twelve.  So, do you like the beer?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I do.  I do.  Although, it's actually a lager.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: huh.  (pause)  What's the difference between beer and lager?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know.  We could look it up.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (nods) Things are about to get wild.
A bar.  Rachel and Phoebe are bringing their drinks from the bar to a sofa.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh God.  Remember the girls' nights we used to have sitting around talking about you and Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God.  It seems like forever ago.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know.  (sighs)   So, what's going on with you and Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, um . . . I don't know.  I mean, for a long time nothing.  But you know, actually right before you picked me up, Ross and I had a . . . ah . . . little thing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!  I love things.  What happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, um, first he told me he liked how I looked.  And, ah, then we had a little . . . um . . . eye-contact.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Eye-contact?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mm-hmm.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I hope you were using protection.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: (with tray and two drinks)  Excuse me.  Um, these are from the two gentlemen at the end of the bar.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.  (to Phoebe) Should we send them something back?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oooh.  Let's do.  Let's send them mashed potatoes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!  Wait!  No, no.  Don't do that!  That's going to make them think they can come over here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So?  What if they do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, we're not here to meet guys.  You have a boyfriend, I have a b. . . baby and a Ross.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but, ah, ah, nothing has to happen.  We're just having fun.  You know, not everything had to go as far as "eye-contact."
Monica and Chandler's apartment.  Chandler is seated in the chair and Monica stands behind the sofa.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, you have to tell Joey that you're not in Tulsa.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't you think it's better for him to think that you're cheating on me, than for him to think that I'm cheating on him?  (Monica tips her head slightly as if asking "Did you hear yourself?")  I heard it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't want him to think that I'm having an affair.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right.  I've got a plan.  I'll go down the fire escape.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, because all good plans start with, "I'll go down the fire escape."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hear me out woman.  I'll go down the fire escape.  Then, I'll wait for a while.  Then, when I come up the stairs, it'll be just like I just got back from Tulsa.  Then, Joey and I will come in and see that there's no guy in here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aren't you afraid that Joey's going to figure all of this out?  (pause)  I heard it.
Chandler runs to the window, opens it, starts out, but returns, casually walking back to his chair.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm just going to wait for a little while.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Scary pigeon's back?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's huge.

Ross and Rachel's apartment.  Three pizza crusts, two bottle caps and the plastic tripod are left in the otherwise empty pizza box.  Mike is making hollow popping noises with his mouth.  He begins to speak, but stops and pops his lips a few more times and takes a drink.  Ross smiles as if he has thought of something to say, but then he stops and sinks back in a slump on the sofa.
The bar.  Two men are chatting with Rachel and Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God.  I can't believe you live in that building.  My grandmother lives in that building.  Ida Green?  No sense of personal space?  Kind of smells like chicken?  Looks like a potato.

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: "Spuds" is your grandmother?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's my bubby!

lines.speaker: Kevin
lines.text: So, we're on our way to a couple of parties.  Um. . . maybe we can get your numbers and give you guys a call if we find something fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. . . I'm sorry.  We weren't really looking for anything to happen with you guys.  I, I have a boyfriend.

lines.speaker: Kevin
lines.text: All right. It's no big deal.

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: So, she has a boyfriend. What is your situation?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, it's complicated. I don't actually have a boyfriend.  But um. . .

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: Then, can I have your number?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (pause) I'm sorry, no.

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: Okay. (They start to walk away.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh sure.  (She pulls a business card from her purse and writes on it.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Reading the card.) Oh my God, you're giving your real number.

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: Okay, thanks. I'll give you a call later tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great.

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: Bye

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bye. (The guys leave.) Wow. So, that's great. You, Bill, Ross, and Emma are going to be so happy together. What were you thinking?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know.  He was cute, and he liked me.  It was an impulse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What about Ross?  What about your moment?  Don't you want to talk to Ross about it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.  No, because I know exactly how the conversation's gonna go.  "Hey Ross, you know, I think we had a moment before."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (lowers voice to imitate Ross) "Yeah."  (Clears throat.)  "Me too."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (as herself) "Well, but I'm not sure I really want to do anything about it."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (as Ross) "Yeah."  (Clears throat twice.) "Me neither." (Clears throat again.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (as herself) "Well, should we just continue to live together and not really tell each other how we're really feeling?"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (as Ross) "Yeah.  That works for me." (Clears throat twice more.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I see what you mean.  By the way, nice Ross imitation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But, your Rachel wasn't whiny enough.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (whining) Wha. . . hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pointing) Better!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, the point is, maybe I should just stop waiting around for moments with Ross, you know?  I should just . . . move on with my life.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?  You're moving on from Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know.  Do I have to decide right now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you kind of just did.  That guy is going to call you tonight.  Ross is going to pick up the phone and that's a pretty clear message.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God, Ross.  Ross is going to pick up the phone.  Oh, I have to get my number back.  (She turns to find Bill, but they have gone.)  Oh my God.  He's gone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (imitating Rachel) "Oh, I have to get my number back.  Oh my God.  He's gone."  (smiles) Dead on.

[Scene: Ross and Rachel's apartment.  Ross and Mike are sitting on the sofa.  Ross is fidgeting with the cuff of his sweater while Mike blows his cheeks out.  Ross blows a piece of fuzz from his finger.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (releases a whoosh of air) Ya know, I'm going to take off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So soon?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.  Well, thanks, ah, thanks for the beer.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ah, you mean lager.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah yeah.  Good times.
Mike leaves.  Ross closes the door behind him.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (In the hall, relieved) Oh.
Ross, inside the door, releases a sign of relief.  Back in the hall, Mike's cell phone rings.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (calling from the bar on her cell phone.) Hey, Mike, it's me.  Listen, is um, is Ross near you?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Uh, no.  I just left.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you have to go back in.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Wha . . .?  Go back?  To the "land where time stands still"?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm so sorry honey, but, okay, Rachel gave this guy her number and, um, she doesn't want Ross to answer the phone.  So, you have to intercept all his calls.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I can't do that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Rachel) He says he can't do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh give me , , ,  (Phoebe gives Rachel the phone.)  Hi, Mike?  Hi.  Listen.  I know this is a lot to ask, but you know what?  If you do this I . . . Phoebe will . . . do anything you want.  Seriously, I'm talking dirty stuff.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. (She takes the phone from Rachel.)  Hello?  Hi.  I'm sorry about her, but she wasn't wrong about the dirty stuff.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: All right.  I'll do it.  (Phoebe gives the thumbs-up sign to Rachel.)  But really, how much dirtier can it get?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (knowingly chuckles) Oh, Mike.  Bye.
Mike knocks on Ross's door.  Ross opens it.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey buddy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, hi.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Um, can I come back in?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (putting his arm up with his hand on the door frame.)  Why?  (He starts to lower his arm.)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (Entering the apartment) I, I was just thinking about how much more we have to talk about.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (whining) But you left.
The hallway and stairs outside Chandler and Monica's apartment.  Chandler enters from the stairs.  Joey is sitting with a baseball bat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow!  That didn't take long.  I thought you said Tulsa was, like a three hour flight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pause) Well, you're forgetting about the time difference.
Joey thinks a moment. Then, he nods.  Joey follows Chandler into the apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (gasp) Chandler!  You're home!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's right.  You're husband's home.  So, now the sex can stop.
Joey gives him and odd look.  Monica gives a similar look.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you saying?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey said that you're in here with another man.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There's no man in here.  How dare you accuse me of that.  (She slaps Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.  All right.  Then, maybe you won't mind if me and my friend take a look around, huh?  (He checks the bathroom shaking the bat.  Then he proceeds to their bedroom.)  Bwa-ah-ah!
Thudding sounds can be heard from the bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) What is he doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (smiling) I arranged some pillows on the bed to look like a guy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (emerging) Bedroom is clear, although you might need some new pillows.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right.  Well, I'll check the guest room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sniffing the air and then Monica.)  Why do I smell men's cologne?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sniffing Joey) I think that's you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sniffs his shirt.)  Oh yeah.  I rubbed a magazine on myself earlier.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's nobody here Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I guess not.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe you thought I was cheating.  (pointing at Joey) You own me an apology.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, right Monica.  I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (suddenly starts issuing him out) Ah, it's an honest mistake.  It could happen to anyone.  All right, see ya.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (spots the suitcase just inside the door where Chandler left it) Whoa, whoa.  Wait a minute.  Wait a minute.  If you just got back from Tulsa, how did your suitcase beat you here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (thinks, then turns to Monica) I climb down the fire escape and you can't put that in the closet?
Ross and Rachel's apartment.  Mike is reading from a book.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So, except for the fermentation process, beer and ale are basically the same thing.  Fascinating isn't it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe you should look up "fascinating."
Phone rings.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I'll get it.  (He lunges across Ross's lap on the sofa to reach the phone.  Ross stares at him with wide eyes.)  Hello?  Ross's place.  Mike speaking.  (pause.  Hands phone to Ross)  It's for you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (takes the phone, but speaks to Mike) I don't understand what just happened here.
Monica and Chandler's apartment " continuing action.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry.  I, I told you I was in Tulsa because I wanted to spend the night with Monica and I, I didn't know . . .  I didn't think you'd understand.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? You think I'm too dumb to understand that a husband needs to be with his wife?  Huh?  Do you think I'm like, "Duh."  (He strikes himself in the head with the bat.  He stands dazed for a moment.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?  (His eyes bug out.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know what to say.  We shouldn't have lied to you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.  I feel so bad.  Is there anything I can do to make it up to you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pause) Yeah, you could go to the game with me, ah, even though I know you said you couldn't.  But then you lied to me and tricked me and gave me a bump on the head.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry.  That's the one thing I can't do.  I promised I'd be with Monica.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (taps chandler on the arm) You can go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You should go to the game.  It's okay.  I want you to.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?  You're gonna be okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I'll be fine.  You know, maybe I'll stay here and practice the art of seduction.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're gonna put on sweats and clean, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's gonna be so hot!  (She kisses him.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, bye.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Have fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks.  (Joey and Chandler exit to the hall.  Joey pulls out the tickets and hands one to Chandler.)  Here's your ticket.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, listen.  I'm never going to lie to you again, okay?  And I want you to know that nobody thinks you're stupid.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks man.
Chandler heads toward the stairs, but makes a turn back to his apartment while looking at the ticket.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where are you going?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Game's tomorrow night Joe.
Chandler goes into the apartment, while Joey checks his ticket and is embarrassed by his stupid mistake.
[Scene: Ross and Rachel's apartment.  Rachel and Phoebe enter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.
Everyone exchanges greetings.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh God.  (He hugs Rachel)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh . . .

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I'm so glad you're back.  (He hugs Phoebe.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow.  So, what did you guys do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you know . . . we just drank some beer and Mike played with the boundaries of normal social conduct.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It's true.  I did.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pause) Well, good bye.
Okays, and good byes are exchanged all around.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That was fun Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know.  That was fun.  (She and Mike exit.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: See you guys.  (She closes the door.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel, lock the door.  Lock the door,  seriously.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh shoot.  I forgot to pay Phoebe for the drinks.  (She exits to the hallway and closes the door behind her.)  Wait, wait.  Sorry.  Did he call?  Did that guy call?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No.  Just his mom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, around 8:30?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Then, again at 9:00?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (nods) uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.
Inside the apartment the phone rings.  Ross answers it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello.  (listens)  Ah, no, she's not here right now.  Can I take a message?  (grabs a pad and pen)  Bill from the bar?  (writes)  Okay, "Bill from the bar."  I'll make sure she gets your number.
He hangs up the phone and tears the note off the pad.  Then, he stops and re-reads it.  Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aaah.  (She goes to tend Emma.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, ah . . . So, how was it?   Uh, did you guys. . . Did you guys have a good time?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it was so much fun.  It felt so good to be out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (holds up the message) Uh, Rach.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (still looking at Emma) Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pauses, then crunches up the note and stuffs it in his pocket.)  Never mind.
[END]
[Tag Scene: Central Perk.  Phoebe and Mike are on the sofa.  Ross enters.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey you guys.  (He sits.)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.  I'll be right back.  I've got to go to the bathroom.  (She rises and exits.)
Ross and Mike glance at each other then both suddenly turn to Phoebe who is gone.  They sheepishly exchange glances.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (finally) Stout.  That's a kind of beer.
Ross smiles slightly.  Then he gives a single nod that lifts him to his feet.  He exits the coffee shop.
[END TAG]
